_api/CalloraVoipSdk.BridgeAudioFormat.ht.title: Enum BridgeAudioFormat | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.BridgeAudioFormat.ht.summary: Enum BridgeAudioFormat Namespace CalloraVoipSdk Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll Audio format delivered to and expected from a media consumer (the "bridge"/tap), such as an AI realtime bridge. When it differs from the negotiated wire codec, the SDK transcodes audio transparently so the consumer sees a single, fixed codec regardless of what the SIP peer negotiated. public enum BridgeAudioFormat Fields Passthrough = 0 Deliver and send the raw negotiated wire payload unchanged (default). The consumer must handle whatever codec was negotiated. Pcmu = 1 Always deliver and accept G.711 µ-law (PCMU, 8 kHz). Inbound wire audio is transcoded to µ-law and outbound µ-law is transcoded to the wire codec. Supported wire codecs: Opus and G.711 (µ-law/A-law); other codecs are delivered as passthrough with a warning.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ca.title: Enum CallState | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ca.summary: Enum CallState Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Lifecycle state of a call. public enum CallState Fields Connected = 3 The call is answered and media is (or is about to be) flowing. Dialing = 1 Outbound call: the INVITE has been sent and the SDK is awaiting a response. Idle = 0 No call activity; the initial state before dialing or ringing. OnHold = 4 The call is on hold; media is suspended in at least one direction. Ringing = 2 The call is ringing — outbound (remote alerting) or inbound (awaiting local accept/reject). Terminated = 6 The call has ended (hung up, rejected, or failed); a terminal state. Transferring = 5 A transfer (blind or attended) is in progress for this call.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.IC.title: Interface ICall | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.IC.summary: Interface ICall Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll A single call and its lifecycle. public interface ICall Remarks Event threading contract. Event handlers run synchronously on the SDK thread that raised the event — this is usually not the thread that created the call. Handlers therefore must not block or perform long-running/synchronous I/O: a blocked handler stalls the SDK path that raised it (SIP signaling or media), delaying every other call on the same line. Off-load real work to your own task or queue, e.g. call.StateChanged += (_, e) => _ = Task.Run(() => Handle(e));. Handlers should also not throw. See each event for the specific thread and whether it is serialized. TransferRequested is the exception to off-loading: it needs a synchronous accept/reject decision, so make that decision quickly inline (do not await long work). Error contract. The action methods split into two error styles by return type. The core lifecycle operations return Task (or Task<TResult> of bool for AttendedTransferAsync(ICall, CancellationToken)) and throw: invalid usage surfaces as InvalidOperationException (wrong state or direction), ArgumentException (invalid argument), and cancellation surfaces as OperationCanceledException; transport, timeout and unexpected transport-layer failures propagate as-is. The extended in-dialog / inbound-response operations return CallActionResult instead and do not throw for foreseeable outcomes: an invalid state or direction, a protocol rejection (for example a SIP 4xx from the peer), an invalid request, cancellation, and transport failures are all reported as a non-success CallActionResult whose Status classifies the cause (see CallActionStatus). These result-returning methods are the ones that involve a SIP round-trip whose negative answer is a normal protocol result rather than a programming error. Per-method tags below state the exact exceptions and result semantics. Properties CallId Stable identifier of this call within the SDK. CallId CallId { get; } Property Value CallId Direction Whether the call was placed locally (Outbound) or received (Inbound). CallDirection Direction { get; } Property Value CallDirection Diversion First Diversion URI (RFC 5806) from an inbound INVITE describing where the call was diverted from; null for outbound calls or when no Diversion header is present. Informational routing history, surfaced as-received. string? Diversion { get; } Property Value string IceSnapshot Read-only ICE (RFC 8445) connectivity snapshot for this leg once candidate-pair selection completes, or null for calls where ICE was not negotiated. Reports the final ICE state and the selected local/remote candidate pair. CallIceSnapshot? IceSnapshot { get; } Property Value CallIceSnapshot Line The phone line this call belongs to. IPhoneLine Line { get; } Property Value IPhoneLine MediaParameters Negotiated media parameters (codec, endpoints). Set once Connected is reached; null before that. CallMediaParameters? MediaParameters { get; } Property Value CallMediaParameters QualitySnapshot Latest quality snapshot derived from RTP/RTCP runtime metrics. Before media starts, this value is an empty baseline snapshot. CallQualitySnapshot QualitySnapshot { get; } Property Value CallQualitySnapshot RemoteAssertedIdentity Peer-asserted caller identity (P-Asserted-Identity, RFC 3325) parsed from an inbound INVITE when the sending peer is trusted, for trunk/PBX routing; null for outbound calls, untrusted peers, or when the header is absent. string? RemoteAssertedIdentity { get; } Property Value string RemoteParty The remote party's SIP URI or address-of-record. string RemoteParty { get; } Property Value string RtpStatistics Latest raw RTP transport statistics (SSRC identifiers, packet/octet counters, RFC 3550 loss and jitter counters) for this leg, or null until the first RTCP reporting interval has produced counters. Complements QualitySnapshot with the underlying uninterpreted counters for diagnostics and billing. CallRtpStatistics? RtpStatistics { get; } Property Value CallRtpStatistics? Task AcceptAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancels the accept; on cancellation OperationCanceledException is thrown. Returns Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException The call is not Inbound, or its state is not Ringing. OperationCanceledException ct was canceled. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded once the INFO is sent; Canceled on cancellation; InvalidRequest for an invalid argument; InvalidState when the transport reports the operation invalid for the current state; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. SendNotifyAsync(string, string, string?, string?, CancellationToken) Sends in-dialog SIP NOTIFY. Does not throw for foreseeable outcomes; the result carries the classification instead. Task<CallActionResult> SendNotifyAsync(string eventType, string subscriptionState, string? contentType = null, string? body = null, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters eventType string The SIP event package the notification belongs to. subscriptionState string The Subscription-State header value. contentType string Optional body content type. body string Optional request body. ct CancellationToken Cancels the operation; reported as Canceled, not thrown. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded when the peer accepts; Rejected when the peer declines the NOTIFY; Canceled on cancellation; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded when the peer accepts; Rejected when the peer declines the OPTIONS; Canceled on cancellation; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. SendSubscribeAsync(string, int, string?, string?, CancellationToken) Sends in-dialog SIP SUBSCRIBE. Does not throw for foreseeable outcomes; the result carries the classification instead. Task<CallActionResult> SendSubscribeAsync(string eventType, int expiresSeconds = 300, string? acceptHeader = null, string? body = null, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters eventType string The SIP event package to subscribe to. expiresSeconds int Requested subscription duration in seconds (default 300). acceptHeader string Optional Accept header value. body string Optional request body. ct CancellationToken Cancels the operation; reported as Canceled, not thrown. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded when the peer accepts; Rejected when the peer declines the SUBSCRIBE; Canceled on cancellation; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. UnholdAsync(CancellationToken) Takes the call off hold and emits a local HoldStateChanged event. Task UnholdAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters ct CancellationToken Accepted for signature symmetry but currently not forwarded to the transport, so the unhold is not cancelled via this token. Returns Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException The call state is not OnHold. Events DtmfReceived Raised when a DTMF tone is received. This event may fire from two threads: the SIP signaling thread (SIP INFO tones) and the media receive thread (RFC 4733 in-band RTP events). _api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ne.title: Enum NetworkQuality | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ne.summary: Enum NetworkQuality Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Coarse, ready-to-use indicator of the media path's health, derived by the SDK from the congestion signal (delay trend + loss). Meant for a simple UI hint or a plugin decision — read it instead of interpreting raw metrics yourself. A call-media value describing the leg, alongside CallQualitySnapshot. public enum NetworkQuality Fields Fair = 1 The path shows mild stress: some loss or a rising delay trend, but still usable. Good = 0 The path has headroom: delay is stable and loss is negligible. Poor = 2 The path is congested: delay is overusing or loss is high — reduce the send bitrate.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.ht.title: Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.ht.summary: Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Classes CallActionResult Unified result model for extended call control operations. CallIceCandidate Represents one ICE candidate associated with a call media leg. CallIceSnapshot Read-only snapshot of ICE (RFC 8445 / RFC 7675) connectivity establishment for one call media leg. Produced once candidate-pair selection completes and exposed via IceSnapshot. It explains which local/remote candidate pair media flows over and why — otherwise an ICE-driven media path or teardown is opaque to the consumer. Non-ICE calls never produce a snapshot (IceSnapshot stays null). CallMediaParameters Negotiated media parameters for one active call leg. Produced by the infrastructure adapter after SDP offer/answer exchange and consumed by the application media orchestrator to set up RTP I/O. CallVideoParameters Negotiated video parameters for one call leg (WebRTC phase 2). Present on Video only when the SDP exchange negotiated an active video m-line with a codec the SDK supports; null keeps the call audio-only. Grouped into one object so parameter enrichment passes video through as a unit. DialOptions Per-call dialing options — a pure Domain value object supplied to DialAsync(string, DialOptions?, CancellationToken). Structs CallId Strongly-typed, immutable identifier for a single call instance. CallQualitySnapshot Public quality snapshot for one call media leg. Values are updated continuously while the call is active and can be used by SDK consumers for routing, alerting, or UI quality indicators. CallRtpStatistics Raw RTP transport statistics for one active call media leg, exposed for diagnostics, billing and troubleshooting. Unlike CallQualitySnapshot — which surfaces derived quality metrics (jitter/loss in ms/%, MOS) — this record carries the underlying RFC 3550 counters (SSRC identifiers, packet/octet counts, RTP-unit jitter) without interpretation. Available on RtpStatistics once media is flowing. DtmfTone Validated DTMF tone (0-9, *, #, A-D). Interfaces ICall A single call and its lifecycle. Enums CallActionStatus Outcome category for extended call control operations. CallDirection Indicates whether a call was initiated by the local party or received from a remote party. CallIceState Public lifecycle state of ICE (RFC 8445) connectivity establishment for one call media leg, surfaced read-only via State. Non-ICE calls never produce an ICE snapshot, so this state is only meaningful when ICE was negotiated for the leg. CallState Lifecycle state of a call. NetworkQuality Coarse, ready-to-use indicator of the media path's health, derived by the SDK from the congestion signal (delay trend + loss). Meant for a simple UI hint or a plugin decision — read it instead of interpreting raw metrics yourself. A call-media value describing the leg, alongside CallQualitySnapshot.

When true (default) a call for the exact username is always accepted, plus — when no InboundNumbers whitelist is set — calls from the trusted registrar peer and domain-addressed calls from an unknown source. When null or empty, the line accepts calls for its exact username, calls delivered by the registrar it registered to, and any number on its registered domain (trunk default). If a challenge does arrive and no password is set, registration fails with a clear error. public string Password { get; init; } Property Value string Port Signaling port; 0 (default) selects the standard port for the chosen Transport. public int Port { get; init; } Property Value int PublicMediaHost Optional public IP address to force into the SDP media connection line (c=) and RTP bind for calls on this line. _api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security.SipDom.title: Class SipDomainCertificateValidator | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security.SipDom.summary: Class SipDomainCertificateValidator Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Validates X.509 certificates against SIP domain identities per RFC 5922 "Domain Certificates in SIP". RFC 5922 §7.1 requires that when a SIP entity establishes a TLS connection, it MUST verify the server certificate contains a subjectAltName (SAN) extension with a value that matches the expected SIP domain. Two SAN entry types are valid: uniformResourceIdentifier — a sip: or sips: URI whose host component matches the SIP domain (case-insensitive DNS comparison). dNSName — a DNS hostname that matches the SIP domain, with wildcard support for the leftmost label (e.g. *.example.com). This validator is intentionally stateless and purely functional so that it can be used from callback contexts (e.g. SslStream certificate validation callbacks) without thread-safety concerns. public static class SipDomainCertificateValidator Inheritance object SipDomainCertificateValidator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods GetSubjectAlternativeNames(X509Certificate2) Extracts all SAN entries from the provided certificate extension. public static IReadOnlyList<string> GetSubjectAlternativeNames(X509Certificate2 certificate) Parameters certificate X509Certificate2 The certificate to inspect. Returns IReadOnlyList<string> A read-only list of SAN string values; empty if the extension is absent. ValidateSipDomain(X509Certificate2, string) Validates that the provided certificate is appropriate for the given SIP domain per RFC 5922 §7.1. public static bool ValidateSipDomain(X509Certificate2 certificate, string sipDomain) Parameters certificate X509Certificate2 The X.509 certificate to validate. Must not be null. sipDomain string The SIP domain to match against (e.g. example.com, sip.example.com). Must not be null or whitespace. Returns bool true if the certificate contains a SAN entry that matches sipDomain; false otherwise. Remarks Per RFC 5922 §7.1: "A SIP implementation MUST check the subjectAltName extension first; if the extension is present and contains the appropriate SIP domain identity, the check succeeds."

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security.TlsCon.title: Class TlsConfiguration | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security.TlsCon.summary: Class TlsConfiguration Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll TLS configuration for SIP transport connections. Supports both outbound (client) and inbound (server) TLS use-cases. Certificate loading is lazy and cached after the first call to GetCertificate(). RFC 5922 compliance: set ExpectedSipDomain to enable domain certificate validation per RFC 5922 §7.1 in addition to the standard chain and hostname checks performed by the TLS stack. public class TlsConfiguration Inheritance object TlsConfiguration Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AcceptUntrustedCertificates When true, the TLS stack accepts server certificates that fail standard chain or hostname validation. Use only in development or testing environments. public bool AcceptUntrustedCertificates { get; init; } Property Value bool CertificatePassword Password for the certificate file, if required. public string? CertificatePassword { get; init; } Property Value string CertificatePath Path to the X.509 certificate file (PFX/P12 or PEM). public string? CertificatePath { get; init; } Property Value string ExpectedSipDomain Optional SIP domain expected in the server certificate's Subject Alternative Name (SAN) extension per RFC 5922 §7.1. When set, ValidatePeerCertificateSipDomain(X509Certificate2) checks that the peer's certificate contains a dNSName or uniformResourceIdentifier (sip:/sips:) SAN that matches this domain. Leave null to skip RFC 5922 SAN validation. public string? ExpectedSipDomain { get; init; } Property Value string Methods GetCertificate() Returns the configured X.509 certificate, loading it from disk on first call. Returns null when no certificate path is configured. public X509Certificate2? GetCertificate() Returns X509Certificate2 ValidatePeerCertificateSipDomain(X509Certificate2) Validates that certificate satisfies the RFC 5922 SIP domain check configured via ExpectedSipDomain. public bool ValidatePeerCertificateSipDomain(X509Certificate2 certificate) Parameters certificate X509Certificate2 The peer X.509 certificate to validate. Returns bool true when ExpectedSipDomain is not set (validation skipped) or when the certificate's SAN matches the expected domain; false when the SAN check fails. Remarks This method is intended to be called from a SslStream certificate validation callback after the standard chain and hostname checks have passed, to add RFC 5922 SIP domain identity verification.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.SdkConfiguration.htm.summary: Class SdkConfiguration Namespace CalloraVoipSdk Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll Top-level SDK configuration: identity, transport security, logging, media/codec behavior, and call-lifecycle timeouts. Supplied once when constructing or registering the SDK. public sealed class SdkConfiguration Inheritance object SdkConfiguration Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AudioDevice Audio device to use for all calls. If left at SilenceAudioDevice and auto selection is enabled, the SDK attempts to load a platform device (Linux/Windows) at runtime. public IAudioDevice AudioDevice { get; init; } Property Value IAudioDevice BridgeAudioFormat Audio format delivered to and expected from the media consumer (bridge/tap). When set to Pcmu, the SDK transcodes between the negotiated wire codec (e.g. Opus) and G.711 µ-law so a µ-law-only consumer works over any negotiated codec. Default Passthrough delivers the raw wire payload. public BridgeAudioFormat BridgeAudioFormat { get; init; } Property Value BridgeAudioFormat DefaultTransport Default SIP signaling transport for outbound requests and the advertised local contact when a target URI does not force one. Defaults to Udp, preserving the prior behavior; set to Tcp/Tls/etc. for TCP- or TLS-only enterprise proxies. public SipTransport DefaultTransport { get; init; } Property Value SipTransport EnableAutomaticAudioDeviceSelection Automatically load a platform audio device when AudioDevice is left at CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Infrastructure.Audio.SilenceAudioDevice. public bool EnableAutomaticAudioDeviceSelection { get; init; } Property Value bool EnableVideo When true, calls negotiate a video stream (WebRTC phase 2, RFC 6184/7741): offers carry an m=video line and inbound video offers are answered. Encoded video frames are exchanged via ICall's media session; the SDK does not encode/decode video itself. Default: false (audio-only). Video is offered only when the offer is not SDES-keyed: an outbound offer under a SDES-offering SrtpPolicy (Optional/Required without OfferDtlsSrtp) stays audio-only — offer DTLS-SRTP or run plain to carry video. Inbound plain/DTLS video offers are answered regardless; SDES video is declined until per-m-line video keying lands. Note: video does not yet gather ICE candidates — with Ice enabled the video m-line carries its port but no candidates, so video needs direct connectivity (no ICE-only peer) until per-component video ICE lands. public bool EnableVideo { get; init; } Property Value bool HangupHeldCallOnMediaSilence Whether the inbound-media timeout also applies to on-hold calls (which legitimately carry no inbound RTP). Default: false (held calls are not torn down). public bool HangupHeldCallOnMediaSilence { get; init; } Property Value bool Ice ICE runtime configuration for NAT traversal and candidate-pair selection. Disabled by default. public IceConfiguration Ice { get; init; } Property Value IceConfiguration InboundMediaTimeout Hang up a connected call whose inbound RTP has been silent this long — a NAT-safe fallback for when a far-end BYE never reaches our in-dialog Contact and the media just stops. Zero disables the check. Default: 15 seconds. public TimeSpan InboundMediaTimeout { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan LoggerFactory Logger factory the SDK logs through; null disables SDK logging. public ILoggerFactory? LoggerFactory { get; init; } Property Value ILoggerFactory MaxConcurrentCallsPerLine Maximum simultaneous calls per phone line. 0 = unlimited. public int MaxConcurrentCallsPerLine { get; init; } Property Value int OfferDtlsSrtp When true, outbound call offers advertise DTLS-SRTP keying (RFC 5763: UDP/TLS/RTP/SAVPF profile plus certificate fingerprint) instead of SDES a=crypto. Inbound DTLS-SRTP offers are answered regardless of this setting. Default: false (SDES per SrtpPolicy). public bool OfferDtlsSrtp { get; init; } Property Value bool PreferredAudioCodecs Ordered audio codec preference by SDP encoding name ("PCMU", "PCMA", "G722", "opus"). When set, SDP offers and answers only include the listed codecs (plus DTMF telephone-event) in this order, and RTP sessions use this preference to pick the primary codec. Opus (RFC 7587, 48 kHz) is opt-in: it is only offered/answered when listed here. Unknown names are ignored; when nothing matches, the SDK default set (G722, PCMA, PCMU) is used. When a listed codec is known but the peer does not offer it, negotiation fails rather than producing an audio-less answer. null keeps defaults. public IReadOnlyList<string>? PreferredAudioCodecs { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<string> PreferredVideoCodecs Ordered video codec preference by SDP encoding name (VP8, H264) when EnableVideo is set. null uses the SDK default (VP8, then H264). Unknown names are ignored. public IReadOnlyList<string>? PreferredVideoCodecs { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<string> SrtpPolicy Default media-encryption policy for calls; defaults to Optional. Overridable per call via DialOptions.UseSrtp. public SrtpPolicy SrtpPolicy { get; init; } Property Value SrtpPolicy Tls TLS settings for secure SIP transport; null uses the transport defaults. public TlsConfiguration? Tls { get; init; } Property Value TlsConfiguration UserAgent Value sent in the SIP User-Agent header; defaults to CalloraVoipSdk/1.0. public string UserAgent { get; init; } Property Value string
